kink.method: hockey-stick LSQ on weekly SOFR-IORB (bp, winsorized 1/99) vs reserves/GDP; grid-searched breakpoint

crunch_windows.reason: $142B auction settlement while reserves sit below the estimated kink
$119B auction settlement while reserves sit below the estimated kink
calendar pressure date while reserves sit below the estimated kink
calendar pressure date while reserves sit below the estimated kink

weather.method: reserves + trailing-8wk WALCL drift - seasonal dTGA (day-of-month median buckets, tax/qtr-end aware); bands = 20/80% backtested cumulative error; auction settlement days >= $90B flagged

tails.method: P99-P50 per rate (bp), 250d z, blend SOFR .5/TGCR .3/BGCR .2, EWMA(5)

echo.method: RMSE similarity of 30d z-trajectories vs pre-episode windows (leads 0-30d)

rvxray.method: TFF futures-only; pair=min(levShort,amLong)xface; DV01 per-contract constants in config; scenarios assume 10% forced unwind vs DVP daily volume

crowding.method: leveraged-fund net position / open interest per contract; z and percentile vs trailing 156w; |z| ranks the board (extremes = crowding)

auctions.method: per-tenor z vs trailing history: 0.5(-btc_z)+0.3(pdShare_z)+0.2(-indirect_z); bills x0.35; EWMA(20)

resonance.method: per calendar mode: slosh = max(SOFR−IORB, event ±1bd) − median(10bd baseline ending 3bd prior); amplification = median slosh last 6 events / prior events; decay = days to give back half the slosh (censored 5d). Modes disjoint; weights: quarter_end 0.3, month_end 0.25, year_end 0.15, mid_month 0.15, tax_date 0.15. ex-max = amplification with the largest recent slosh removed (one-event sensitivity); modes with n<10 carry a low-n flag

hydrophone.method: absorption ratio = top-2 PC variance share of 120bd standardized daily changes (Kritzman), sampled 5bd; edges = max |lag-k xcorr| k=1..3, |r|>=0.3

warehouse.method: NY Fed PD stats: net outright UST positions summed over bills + coupon buckets ($B, weekly, published T+9); saturation = percentile vs full spliced history; long-end = share in >7y buckets

basins.anchor: SONIA level (no keyless daily policy anchor)
INR/USD (FX channel only — rates feed pending)

basins.out_of_scope: Japan (TONA), China, Russia, African markets: no keyless daily feed that meets the provenance bar — excluded rather than faked. India rides the FX channel only (CCIL = HTML, RBI DBIE = broken SSL, probed 2026-07-07); a rates anchor joins when a qualifying feed exists
basins.method: tide = top-2 PC variance share of 120bd standardized daily changes across basins+channels (sampled 5bd, pctl vs own history); edges = max |lag-k xcorr| k=1..3, |r|>=0.25; confession = NY Fed USD swap ops ex small-value tests + H.4.1 outstanding

TGA.note: innovations vs the fiscal seasonal model — burns = unscheduled cash moves (debt-ceiling games, buyback shifts)

RRP.note: burns = the shock absorber refilling/draining outside its calendar rhythm

WALCL.note: burns = balance-sheet policy changes (QT stop, RMP pace) vs the trailing 26w drift

stationkeeping.method: orbit-determination transfer: propagate expected state (fiscal seasonal / calendar-bucket / trailing drift), standardize one-step innovations by rolling MAD (floor $0.5B), two-sided CUSUM k=0.5, h=6.0 daily / 4.0 weekly; alarm = unmodeled burn. Doubles as the Weather model's health monitor. Context, not composite.

moorings.caveat: USDC/DAI pegs are spot values (no free keyless history); USDT peg history is Coinbase daily closes; context for the dollar system, not weighted into the Seiche Index
moorings.method: peg dev bp vs $1 (flag |dev| ≥ 30.0bp); USDT |dev| robust-z 250d; offshore demand = Δ total circulation (drain flag ≤ -3.0%/30d); canary = BTC 10d realized vol z + largest weekend move (crypto trades when funding markets sleep)

sonar.method: robust z = (last − median) / (1.4826·MAD) over trailing 250 obs, on level and 1d change; flag |z| ≥ 2.5

history.method: Seiche-lite: expanding-window standardization only (no look-ahead); components tails/kink-proxy/confession/rvxray/auctions/buffers with live composite weights renormalized; weather/resonance/hydrophone/warehouse are live-only and excluded (stated, not hidden); final-vintage data

tell.method: Tell = rolling-750d percentile of Seiche-lite minus weighted rolling percentiles of VIX/HY OAS/IG OAS/10y realized vol (weights {'VIX': 0.35, 'HY_OAS': 0.3, 'IG_OAS': 0.15, 'RATES_VOL': 0.2}); divergence is a signal, not stress evidence

playbook.caveat: historical distributions conditioned on the current state; overlapping forward windows mean n_days overstates independent samples (n_independent ≈ n_days/horizon shown); native units; not investment advice
playbook.method: state = Seiche-lite regime × Tell bucket; forward outcomes over [5, 20] bd on matching historical days

validation.note: model skill vs naive is only 0.026 on LOO-CV — statistically indistinguishable from naive, so the naive same-mode median is published

turn.method: OLS on 7 features frozen T-5bd before each historical turn; LOO-CV vs naive same-mode trailing median; band = LOO residual 20/80%

event_capture.precision_note: day-level; alert days are serially correlated — use run-level

caveats: expanding-window standardization only — no look-ahead in the signal
alert threshold fixed in config, not fitted to outcomes
final-vintage data (weekly H.4.1 aggregates are lightly revised; daily market prints effectively are not)
overlapping forward windows: n_independent ≈ n_days / horizon is shown
Seiche-lite excludes live-only engines (weather/resonance/hydrophone/warehouse) — the live index has MORE information than this backtest
event count is small — Wilson 95% intervals are printed next to every rate; run-level precision is the load-bearing number (alert days are serially correlated)
the lite index contains spread/tail terms and the event is a spread spike — see the ORTHOGONAL test for the same claim with the target's own variables removed
backtest.method: event = SOFR−IORB ≥ +10.0bp vs trailing 5d median (clusters collapsed); alert = Seiche-lite expanding pctl ≥ 80.0; capture window 5bd

event_capture.precision_note: day-level; alert days are serially correlated — use run-level

orthogonal.why: same event-capture test with the target's own variable family removed from the signal (no spread, no tails) — kink-proxy/confession/rvxray/auctions/buffers only

ml.verdict: does not out-rank the rule-based index (0.806 vs 0.855); probability LEVELS do not beat climatology — use for ranking/alerting, not as literal odds

utility.unit: net caught-events per year (false alarms charged at cost)

caveats: events are rare — AUROC on few positives deserves humility
walk-forward only; no shuffled CV (leakage on time series)
5bd boundary embargo: training rows whose labels see into the test block are dropped
features trailing-only; labels final-vintage (same caveat as PROOF)
probabilities are raw model outputs; check the reliability table before trusting a level
ml.method: HistGradientBoosting (depth 3), expanding walk-forward refits every 42bd after 500d warmup, 5bd embargo; target = funding event (+10.0bp vs 5d median) within 5bd

orthogonal.verdict: AUROC 0.797 (no rule comparator in this feature set); probability levels beat climatology

utility.unit: net caught-events per year (false alarms charged at cost)
